dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7332 IH: Whale CHARTS Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Matsui (for herself, Ms. Malliotakis, Ms. Dexter, Mr. Buchanan, Ms. Elfreth, Mr. Carter of Georgia, Ms. Bonamici, Mr. Rutherford, Mr. Quigley, Mrs. Radewagen, Mr. Huffman, Ms. King-Hinds, Mr. Moulton, Mr. Bilirakis, Mr. Krishnamoorthi, Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia, Ms. Brownley, Mr. Fitzpatrick, Mr. Smith of Washington, Mr. Moylan, Mr. Suozzi, and Ms. Mace) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources, and in addition to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 to provide for a mapping, surveying, monitoring, and mitigation program for migratory whales and other large cetaceans.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Whale CHARTS Act of 2026.

section.header: Mapping, surveying, monitoring, and mitigation program for migratory whales and other large cetaceans

subsection.text: Section 11303 of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 (16 U.S.C. 1391) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by striking NEAR REAL-TIME MONITORING AND MITIGATION PROGRAM FOR and inserting MAPPING, SURVEYING, MONITORING, AND MITIGATION PROGRAM FOR MIGRATORY WHALES AND OTHER;
in subsection (a)—

subparagraph.text: by striking near real-time monitoring and inserting mapping, surveying, monitoring,;
by striking (referred to in this section as the Program); and
by striking threatened or endangered and inserting migratory whales and other large;

paragraph.text: in subsection (b)—

subparagraph.text: by inserting migratory whales and other before large cetaceans posed;
by inserting migratory whales and other before large cetaceans through; and
by striking the use of near real-time location monitoring and location information and inserting the following:

quoted-block.text: increased and improved data collection, including—

paragraph.text: mapping and surveying of migratory whales;
monitoring of large cetaceans; and
the development of detection technologies for migratory whales and other large cetaceans.
by redesignating subsections (e) through (i) as subsections (g) through (k), respectively;
by redesignating subsection (c) as subsection (f);
in subsection (f), as so redesignated—

subparagraph.text: in the heading, by striking REQUIREMENTS and inserting NEAR REAL-TIME MONITORING AND MITIGATION SUBPROGRAM FOR LARGE CETACEANS;
by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (6) as subparagraphs (A) through (F), respectively (and by adjusting the margins of such subparagraphs accordingly);
by striking The Program and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: The Subprogram

subparagraph.text: by inserting before paragraph (3), as so redesignated, the following:

paragraph.text: The Under Secretary, in coordination with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, shall design and deploy a cost-effective, efficient, and results-oriented near real-time monitoring and mitigation subprogram for threatened or endangered cetaceans.

The purpose of the Subprogram shall be to reduce the risk to large cetaceans posed by vessel collisions and to minimize other impacts on large cetaceans through the use of near real-time location monitoring and location information.

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3)(F), as so redesignated, by striking subsection (d) and inserting paragraph (4);

paragraph.text: in subsection (d)(4)(B)(ii)(II)(bb), to read as follows:

subsection.text: a strategic plan to expand the pilot project to provide near real-time monitoring and mitigation measures to additional large cetaceans of concern for which such measures would reduce risk of serious injury or death and in important feeding, breeding, calving, rearing, or migratory habitats of large cetaceans that co-occur with areas of high risk of mortality or serious injury from vessel strikes or disturbance;

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (d) as paragraph (4) of subsection (f), as so redesignated (and by adjusting the margin of paragraph (4) accordingly);
in subsection (f)(4), as so redesignated—

subparagraph.text: in the heading, by striking PILOT PROJECT and inserting PILOT PROJECT;

by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (4) as subparagraphs (A) through (D), respectively (and by redesignating any subdivisions under such subparagraphs (as so redesignated) to include adjusting the margins of such subparagraphs (and their subdivisions) accordingly);
by striking Program each place it appears and inserting Subprogram;
in subparagraph (C), as so redesignated, by striking paragraph (2) each place it appears and inserting subparagraph (B); and
in subparagraph (D)—

clause.text: in clause (i)(II), as so redesignated, by striking clause (i) and inserting subclause (I); and

subclause.text: by striking clause (i) and inserting subclause (I); and
in item (aa), by striking subparagraph (A) and inserting clause (i);

paragraph.text: by inserting after subsection (b) the following:

The Under Secretary, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission, shall produce for stocks, populations, or species, as the Under Secretary determines appropriate, of migratory whales within waters under the jurisdiction of the United States—

subparagraph.text: distribution maps of high accuracy and spatial and temporal resolution; and
predictive distribution maps of high accuracy and spatial and temporal resolution that take into account expected future environmental conditions.

paragraph.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Under Secretary—

subparagraph.text: shall—

clause.text: use the best scientific information available;
conduct or support research to identify and fill knowledge gaps, which may include—

subclause.text: developing novel sources of data, such as passive acoustic monitoring, detection through satellite or drone imagery, and other on-water asset-based sensors; and

item.text: to combine different sources of migratory whale detection data; or
to improve migratory whale distribution and habitat modeling;

clause.text: include in each map produced under such paragraph, as applicable—
as needed, use novel sources of data described in clause (ii)(I) in addition to current sources of data, such as visual detection of migratory whales using aerial- and vessel-based surveys;
make each map produced under such paragraph available—

item.text: electronic and machine readable formats that can be integrated into on-board vessel navigation systems, including—

subitem.text: electronic navigational products of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, as appropriate;
an online platform for the general public; and
in coordination with the Commandant of the Coast Guard, carriage requirements set by the Commandant of the Coast Guard for commercial or recreational vessels;

item.text: in coordination with the Commandant of the Coast Guard, other formats not specified under item (aa) that may be required in carriage requirements set by the Commandant of the Coast Guard for commercial or recreational vessels, as appropriate; and
other formats the Under Secretary determines appropriate; and

subclause.text: to other entities that produce products or publications included in the carriage requirements set by the Commandant of the Coast Guard for commercial or recreational vessels, as appropriate; and

clause.text: require the heads of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration line offices to coordinate the work of such offices, as needed; and

subparagraph.text: may, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission and non-Federal stakeholders, including State and local governments, research institutions, industry groups, and nongovernmental organizations, determine—

clause.text: which stocks, populations, or species of migratory whales to prioritize under paragraph (1); and
whether to prioritize the collection and aggregation of new data or the processing, analysis, aggregation, and modeling of existing data.

paragraph.text: Not later than 3 years after the date of the enactment of this subsection and triennially thereafter, the Under Secretary shall, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission and non-Federal stakeholders, including State and local governments, research institutions, industry groups, and nongovernmental organizations, carry out a review regarding whether the Under Secretary should update any map produced under paragraph (1) or produce any new map under paragraph (1).

The Under Secretary, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission, shall conduct surveys specific to understudied stocks, populations, or species, as the Under Secretary determines appropriate, of migratory whales within waters under the jurisdiction of the United States to estimate the abundance and distribution of each such stock, population, or species.
In carrying out paragraph (1), the Under Secretary—

subparagraph.text: shall—

clause.text: identify in each survey conducted under such paragraph, as applicable, potential—
indicate in each such survey the degree of scientific certainty associated with the identification of each area described in subclauses (I) through (V) of clause (i); and
as needed, use current and novel sources of data; and

subparagraph.text: may—

clause.text: as the Under Secretary determines appropriate, collect opportunistic data on other marine species; and

determine, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission and non-Federal stakeholders, including State and local governments, research institutions, industry groups, and nongovernmental organizations, which stocks, populations, or species of migratory whales to prioritize under paragraph (1).

paragraph.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this subsection, the Under Secretary shall establish a competitive grant program to award amounts, on an annual basis, to eligible entities for the eligible uses described in paragraph (5).

The Under Secretary shall enter into a cooperative agreement with the Foundation pursuant to the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation Establishment Act (16 U.S.C. 3701 et seq.) for the Foundation to manage and administer the grant program.

To be eligible for a grant pursuant to this subsection, an eligible entity shall submit to the Foundation an application in such form, at such time, and containing such information as the Foundation determines appropriate.

subparagraph.text: After the Under Secretary enters into a cooperative agreement with the Foundation under paragraph (2)—

clause.text: for each fiscal year, the Under Secretary shall provide to the Foundation the amounts made available under this section in an advance payment of the entire amount on October 1, or as soon as practicable thereafter, of each such fiscal year; and
the Foundation shall invest and reinvest excess such amounts for the benefit of the grant program.

subparagraph.header: Application of national fish and wildlife foundation establishment act
subparagraph.text: Amounts received by the Foundation under this subsection shall be subject to the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation Establishment Act (16 U.S.C. 3701 et seq.), except for section 10(a) of that Act (16 U.S.C. 3709(a)).

paragraph.text: Funds made available for award under this subsection may be spent to develop, assess, and carry out activities that reduce lethal and sub-lethal interactions between ocean users and migratory whales and other large cetaceans, including—

subparagraph.text: funding research to develop, deploy, or test innovative detection technologies that reduce or eliminate harmful interactions between ocean users and migratory whales and other large cetaceans;
efforts to enhance awareness of existing management measures for migratory whales and other large cetaceans;
developing on-the-water approaches to support the coexistence of ocean users and migratory whales and other large cetacean species;

funding the expansion of infrastructure and capacity to disseminate management and other relevant information that reduces harmful interactions between ocean users and migratory whales and other large cetaceans, especially such interactions that can lead to lethal or sub-lethal injury; and
other uses the Foundation, in consultation with the Under Secretary and relevant eligible entities, determines appropriate.

paragraph.text: In awarding grants under this subsection, the Foundation shall give priority to applications—

subparagraph.text: with a substantial likelihood of reducing lethal and sub-lethal interactions between ocean users and migratory whales and other large cetaceans;
that include cooperation among ocean users; and
that demonstrate, or have the potential to provide, economic benefits to small businesses reliant upon fishing, tourism, and maritime-related activities based in the United States.

An eligible entity that is awarded a grant under this subsection may not use such grant to distribute resources to an entity or individual that is not a United States person, except as provided in subparagraph (B).

An eligible entity that is awarded a grant under this subsection may use such grant to distribute resources to a partnership that includes an entity or individual that is not a United States person if the resources are distributed directly to a partner in the partnership that is a United States person.

paragraph.text: in subsection (k), as so redesignated, to read as follows:
There are authorized to be appropriated to the Under Secretary for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2030—

subparagraph.text: to carry out subsection (c), $2,000,000;
to carry out subsection (d), $1,000,000; and
to carry out subsection (f), $5,000,000.

paragraph.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Under Secretary to carry out subsection (e), $10,000,000 to remain available until expended.

Each fiscal year, of the amounts made available to carry out subsection (e), the Foundation may use not more than 5 percent or $80,000, whichever is greater, of such amounts for administrative expenses.
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this subsection, and every 3 years thereafter, the Under Secretary, in consultation with the Marine Mammal Commission, shall submit to Congress and make available to the public a report regarding—

paragraph.text: activities carried out under subsections (c) and (d);
knowledge gaps identified in carrying out subsection (c);
progress made in filling knowledge gaps identified in carrying out subsection (c); and
the results and effectiveness of projects carried out with a grant awarded under the grant program.
The Under Secretary shall carry out this section notwithstanding any planning or ongoing actions, including rulemakings, under any other provision of law.
Nothing in this section may be construed to—

subparagraph.text: affect the authority or duty of the Under Secretary to issue timely regulations under any provision of law; or
allow the Under Secretary to delay any action, order, or rulemaking process under any provision of law.

paragraph.text: The term eligible entity means—

subparagraph.text: a State, regional, local, or Tribal government;
a non-profit organization or research institution with expertise in monitoring or detecting migratory whales or other large cetaceans;

any individual or entity the Under Secretary and the Foundation jointly determine appropriate, including ocean users in the fishing, tourism, fishing tackle manufacturing, boating, shipping, marine electronics, ship piloting, vessel towing, and other maritime sectors; and
a consortium of 2 or more entities described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (C).

paragraph.text: The term Foundation means the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation.
The term grant program means the grant program established under subsection (e)(1).
The term Marine Mammal Commission means the Marine Mammal Commission established by section 201(a) of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 (16 U.S.C. 1401(a)).
The term migratory whale means all species within either—

subparagraph.text: the suborder Mysticeti; or
the genus Physeter.

paragraph.text: The term pilot project means the pilot monitoring and mitigation project established under subsection (f)(4)(A).
The term Program means the program established under subsection (a).
The term Subprogram means the subprogram established under subsection (f)(1).
The term United States person has the meaning given the term in section 7701(a)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

paragraph.header: Waters under the jurisdiction of the united states
paragraph.text: The term waters under the jurisdiction of the United States has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 (16 U.S.C. 1362).

subsection.text: Section 117 of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 (16 U.S.C. 1386) is amended by adding at the end the following:

In preparing, publishing, or reviewing a stock assessment under this section, the Secretary shall consider each applicable distribution map produced under section 11303(c) of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 (16 U.S.C. 1391(c)).
